title: Slow-cooker chicken tikka masala
subtitle: The British curry house classic, made at home
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs slow-cooked in a spiced tomato and cream sauce with onion, garlic, ginger, garam masala, and smoked paprika for the characteristic red colour. Six hours builds the sauce to a depth the 20-minute version misses. Serve with basmati rice and naan.
sourceNotes: Chicken tikka masala is widely claimed as one of Britain's national dishes. It was developed in British curry restaurants in the 1970s as an adaptation of Punjabi chicken tikka — the masala sauce was added to suit the British preference for dishes served in a sauce. The slow cooker version skips the tikka marinade and grill stage, producing a home-cooked version of the sauce that is accessible on a weeknight.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the cream. Add double cream when reheating.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe and freeze in portions without the cream.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cook the day before and reheat gently. Add cream just before serving.

content.text: The smoked paprika is responsible for the red colour — tikka masala is visually distinct from butter chicken because of the paprika and higher quantity of tomato in the sauce. Bloom the spices in oil on the hob before adding to the slow cooker; this step cannot be skipped and takes two minutes.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a wide pan over medium heat. Fry the {{onion}} for 5 minutes. Add the {{garlic}} and {{ginger-root}} and cook for 1 minute. Add the {{garam-masala}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{coriander-ground}}, {{chilli-powder}}, and {{tomato-puree}} and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the chicken is tender. Stir in the {{double-cream}} and taste. Adjust seasoning.

items.symptom: Colour pale orange rather than deep red
items.cause: Not enough smoked paprika or tomato puree
items.fix: Increase the smoked paprika to 3 teaspoons and the tomato puree to 3 tablespoons. The red colour comes from the combination of the two

items.cause: Cream not added or too little
items.fix: Stir in the double cream at the end — it rounds the acidity from the tomatoes and gives the sauce its characteristic body

items.symptom: Spice level too strong after cooking
items.cause: UK chilli powder is pure chilli — much hotter than the US blend
items.fix: Start with 0.25 teaspoon and taste. The spice builds over 6 hours. Add more at the end if needed

content.text: Chicken tikka masala is cooked more in British homes than any other curry. The slow cooker version produces a sauce that is richer than a 20-minute weeknight version because the tomatoes reduce and concentrate over six hours. Serve with basmati rice, naan, and a cucumber raita to cut through the richness.
